retrieved_docs.title: Cryoglobulinemic Vasculitis(Archived) -- Differential Diagnosis
retrieved_docs.content: Henoch Schlein purpura (HSP) causes palpable purpura in lower extremities but can be differentiated by immunofluorescence of skin biopsy. In HSP, immunofluorescence usually shows the deposition of IgA and not immune complexes.

retrieved_docs.title: Petechiae -- Differential Diagnosis
retrieved_docs.content: Ecchymosis Palpable purpura Retiform purpura

retrieved_docs.title: IgA Vasculitis (Henoch-Schönlein Purpura) -- Differential Diagnosis
retrieved_docs.content: The differential diagnosis of IgAV includes the following: IgA nephropathy Acute renal failure Acute glomerulonephritis Idiopathic thrombocytopenic purpura Disseminated intravascular coagulation Thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura Hemolytic uremic syndrome Meningococcal meningitis Hypersensitivity vasculitis Systemic lupus erythematosus Polyarteritis nodosa Bacterial endocarditis Inflammatory bowel disease Wegener granulomatosis Rocky Mountain spotted fever

retrieved_docs.title: Poststreptococcal Glomerulonephritis -- Differential Diagnosis
retrieved_docs.content: Henoch Schonlein purpura (HSP): The typical presentation is palpable purpura, renal failure, gastrointestinal and musculoskeletal manifestations. Have normal complement levels.

retrieved_docs.title: IgA Vasculitis (Henoch-Schönlein Purpura) -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: The diagnosis of IgA vasculitis is made based on the presence of petechiae (without thrombocytopenia) or palpable purpura that predominantly affects the lower limbs plus at least one of the following four characteristics: [1] Abdominal pain Arthralgia or arthritis Renal involvement (proteinuria, red blood cell casts, or hematuria) Proliferative glomerulonephritis or leukocytoclastic vasculitis with predominant deposition of IgA on histology

retrieved_docs.title: Cryoglobulinemia -- Differential Diagnosis
retrieved_docs.content: IgA vasculitis (Henoch-Schönlein purpura); Henoch Schlein purpura (HSP) causes palpable purpura in lower extremities but can be differentiated by immunofluorescence of skin biopsy. In HSP, immunofluorescence usually shows the deposition of IgA and not immune complexes.

retrieved_docs.title: IgA Vasculitis (Henoch-Schönlein Purpura) -- History and Physical
retrieved_docs.content: The classic presentation of IgA vasculitis includes palpable purpura, gastrointestinal complaints, arthralgias, and renal involvement. Patients may present with any of the following signs or symptoms: Rash Fatigue Headache Fever Joint pain Subcutaneous edema Diarrhea Hematemesis Abdominal pain Vomiting Rectal bleeding Scrotal edema

retrieved_docs.title: Acute Abdomen -- Differential Diagnosis
retrieved_docs.content: IgA vasculitis;

retrieved_docs.title: Petechiae -- Etiology -- Vasculitis and Inflammatory Conditions
retrieved_docs.content: Henoch-Schonlein purpura (HSP) Systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE)

retrieved_docs.title: Microscopic Polyangiitis -- History and Physical -- Skin Manifestations
retrieved_docs.content: Skin lesions are observed in 30% to 60% of patients with MPA, and these lesions are the initial presenting feature in 15% to 30% of cases. [22] [23] These skin manifestations include palpable purpura, livedo reticularis, urticaria, nodules, and ulcers with necrosis. Additionally, skin manifestations have been associated with joint pain in patients with MPA (see Image. Typical Skin Manifestations Observed in Microscopic Polyangiitis). [24]

retrieved_docs.title: Pernio -- Differential Diagnosis
retrieved_docs.content: Other conditions, particularly vasospastic conditions, that the clinician should consider in the differential include: Raynaud phenomenon Acrocyanosis Cryoglobulinemia Cold panniculitis

retrieved_docs.title: Purpura Fulminans -- History and Physical
retrieved_docs.content: The skin findings of purpura fulminans have a characteristic appearance and evolution. Purpura fulminans begins with erythema, which develops irregular central areas of blue-black hemorrhagic necrosis. In some cases, vesicles and bulla form. The affected skin initially is painful and indurated, but in the later stages, there may be a total loss of sensation. Secondary infection of gangrenous tissue may occur. Necrosis may extend to deeper tissues.

retrieved_docs.title: Type III Hypersensitivity Reaction -- Evaluation -- IgA Vasculitis
retrieved_docs.content: Clinicians biopsy the affected organ, such as the kidney or the skin, in patients with atypical presentations. The presence of leukocytoclastic vasculitis and a predominance of IgA deposition confirms the diagnosis. Additional studies include a platelet count, PT, PTT, and bleeding time to exclude other diagnoses that cause purpura. Urinalysis and serum creatinine evaluate the level of kidney involvement. Imaging, such as plain radiographs and ultrasound, may be necessary for abdominal and testicular pain.

retrieved_docs.title: Leukocytoclastic Vasculitis -- Differential Diagnosis
retrieved_docs.content: Skin biopsy can easily differentiate benign pigmented purpura from leukocytoclastic vasculitis given the lack of features of vasculitis such as fibrinoid necrosis and vessel wall disruption by the inflammatory infiltrates. [12]

retrieved_docs.title: Type III Hypersensitivity Reaction -- Evaluation -- IgA Vasculitis
retrieved_docs.content: The diagnosis of IgA vasculitis is primarily clinical, based on the presence of palpable purpura of the lower extremities and buttocks in the absence of thrombocytopenia or coagulopathy, plus 2 or 3 of the following clinical features: Arthritis or arthralgia; Abdominal pain; and Kidney disease. [32] [33]

retrieved_docs.title: Type III Hypersensitivity Reaction -- Differential Diagnosis
retrieved_docs.content: Purpura fulminans

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 3 -- Community 2447
retrieved_docs.content: Necrotizing glomerulonephritis is classified as a form of اعتلال كبيبات الكلى (glomerular disease) and is frequently encountered as a component of متلازمة رئوية كلوية (pulmonary‑renal syndrome). As a severe, necrotizing inflammation of the renal glomeruli, it represents a specific subtype within the broader category of glomerular pathology. Its association with pulmonary‑renal syndrome highlights the clinical pattern in which simultaneous lung hemorrhage and rapidly progressive glomerulonephritis occur, often due to small‑vessel vasculitis or autoimmune mechanisms. Recognizing necrotizing glomerulonephritis as both a distinct glomerular disease and a manifestation of pulmonary‑renal syndrome is essential for accurate diagnosis, prompt immunosuppressive treatment, and coordinated care of the intertwined renal and pulmonary complications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 29 -- Level 1 -- Community 52
retrieved_docs.content: range of autoimmune hematologic and rheumatologic disorders are interconnected within the broader context of circulatory and connective‑tissue diseases, informing comprehensive patient evaluation and management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 3 -- Community 2041
retrieved_docs.content: Aplastic anemia, non‑regenerative anemia, arthritis, hemolytic anemia that is not caused by autoimmunity, and coronary artery inflammation are all classified within the broader category of immune disorders/autoimmune conditions. This grouping reflects the shared underlying involvement of the immune system in the pathogenesis of these diverse diseases, whether through direct autoimmune attack on blood‑forming tissues, inflammatory processes affecting joints, or vascular inflammation of the coronary arteries. Recognizing these entities as members of the immune/autoimmune disorder spectrum helps clinicians consider common immunologic mechanisms, guide diagnostic evaluation, and select therapeutic strategies that target immune dysregulation across these seemingly disparate clinical presentations.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 2 -- Community 1148
retrieved_docs.content: Systemic lupus erythematosus (الذئبة الحمراء المجموعية) is clinically associated with other circulatory disease, and this category of circulatory disease is classified as a subtype of connective tissue disorder (اضطراب الأنسجة الضامة). This relationship underscores that cardiovascular manifestations observed in lupus are part of the broader spectrum of connective tissue diseases, highlighting the importance of integrated cardiovascular evaluation and management in patients with systemic lupus erythematosus.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1795
retrieved_docs.content: The skin disorder حُمامَى is linked to other inflammatory conditions of the skin, indicating that it shares pathogenic mechanisms and clinical associations with additional inflammatory dermatoses. This “associated_with” relationship highlights the relevance of recognizing حُمامَى within the broader spectrum of skin inflammation for accurate diagnosis and comprehensive patient management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 1973
retrieved_docs.content: Symptoms are closely related to, and may be considered synonymous with, the signs and symptoms of ill‑defined conditions, reflecting the overlap between patient‑reported experiences and observable clinical manifestations in poorly characterized disorders. In this framework, diseases and injuries are placed under the broader category of signs and symptoms of ill‑defined conditions, indicating that many inadequately defined pathologies present with nonspecific clinical findings that are captured by this umbrella term. Together, these relationships underscore the interconnected nature of clinical presentations (symptoms) and the underlying, often vague, disease or injury processes, highlighting the importance of comprehensive evaluation when managing patients with ill‑defined conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 33 -- Level 1 -- Community 250
retrieved_docs.content: to blood loss, illustrating the interplay between hemorrhagic manifestations and resultant hematologic deficits. Collectively, these relationships demonstrate how hemodialysis can precipitate acute, sudden‑onset disorders and chronic complications, how necrotizing glomerulonephritis represents a severe, systemic glomerular disease, and how renal functional impairment, specific glomerular lesions, autoimmune conditions, drug‑induced reactions, and bleeding‑related anemias intersect within medical classification systems to inform comprehensive patient assessment and coordinated care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 3 -- Community 2276
retrieved_docs.content: The category of autoimmune disorders (اضطرابات مناعية/المناعة الذاتية) encompasses several distinct conditions, including التهاب المفاصل (arthritis), فقر الدم الانحلالي المكتسب (acquired hemolytic anemia), فقر الدم الانحلالي من النوع البارد (cold‑agglutinin hemolytic anemia), and التهاب الشريان التاجي (coronary artery inflammation). Each of these diseases is characterized by an inappropriate immune response that targets the body’s own tissues, leading to joint inflammation, destruction of red blood cells, or inflammation of the coronary arteries. Recognizing that these diverse clinical entities share a common autoimmune basis aids clinicians in diagnosing, monitoring, and applying immunomodulatory therapies across this spectrum of immune‑mediated illnesses.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 29 -- Level 4 -- Community 2867
retrieved_docs.content: Other diseases of veins and lymphatics have a broader relationship with an unspecified disorder of the circulatory system, indicating that these venous and lymphatic conditions are encompassed within the larger category of undefined circulatory system disturbances. This broader connection highlights the importance of considering venous and lymphatic pathologies when evaluating any non‑specific circulatory disorder, as they may share underlying mechanisms or contribute to the overall clinical picture. Recognizing this relationship can aid clinicians in comprehensive assessment and management of patients presenting with ambiguous circulatory symptoms.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 4 -- Community 2848
retrieved_docs.content: حَمْلٌ بُوقِيّ and ربط البوق are medical concepts that frequently co‑occur, indicating that the presence of حَمْلٌ بُوقِيّ is often associated with the finding or procedure ربط البوق. This consistent association suggests that clinicians should be aware of the link between these two entities, as recognizing one may prompt evaluation for the other, thereby informing diagnosis and guiding appropriate patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 4 -- Level 2 -- Community 1239
retrieved_docs.content: The collection “اضطرابات مناعية/المناعة الذاتية (SMQ)” comprises a range of hematologic and inflammatory conditions that share an autoimmune basis. Aplastic anemia (فقر الدم الوبيل) is considered an autoimmune disorder, as are autoimmune hemolytic anemia (فقر الدم الانحلالي الناجم عن المناعة الذاتية) and cold‑type hemolytic anemia (فقر الدم الانحلالي من النوع البارد). Arthritis (التهاب المفاصل) and coronary artery inflammation (التهاب الشريان التاجي) are also members of this autoimmune group, illustrating the systemic nature of immune‑mediated disease. Although non‑immune hemolytic anemia (فقر دم انحلالي ليس ناجمًا عن المناعة الذاتية) is listed among these entities, it does not result from autoantibody‑mediated red‑cell destruction; its inclusion reflects clinical overlap and underscores the importance of distinguishing immune from non‑immune mechanisms when evaluating hemolysis. Recognizing that these diverse conditions are linked by an underlying autoimmune process helps clinicians diagnose, monitor, and manage patients with immune‑driven pathology more effectively.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 1 -- Community 102
retrieved_docs.content: with SLE or connective tissue disorders. The laboratory test PBI is related to اضطراب الدم. نقص غلوبيولين الدم (blood‑protein deficiency) is a child condition of اضطراب الدم and serves as a parent condition for immunity disorders, highlighting the link between global immune dysfunction and specific globulin deficiencies. كثرة الخلايا البدينة (eosinophilia) is a parent category of اضطراب الدم. Diseases and injuries are grouped under اضطراب الدم, and سرطان المعدة is a specific condition within سرطان المعدة. The overlapping clinical presentations across these interconnected disorders are emphasized by the inclusion of signs and symptoms of ill‑defined conditions as related and possibly synonymous entities.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 2 -- Community 740
retrieved_docs.content: central concept linked to hypertension, specific renal syndromes (hemolytic‑uremic syndrome, lupus nephritis, crescentic glomerulonephritis), procedural interventions, and broader interpretations of impairment, emphasizing the importance of integrated diagnostic coding, etiologic understanding, and multidisciplinary management in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 3 -- Community 2177
retrieved_docs.content: Necrotizing glomerulonephritis is a severe, necrotizing form of التهاب كبيبات الكلى (glomerulonephritis), representing a specific subtype within the broader category of glomerular inflammation. This disease is closely associated with متلازمة رئوية كلوية (pulmonary‑renal syndrome), a clinical entity in which acute lung involvement—often pulmonary hemorrhage—occurs together with rapid deterioration of renal function. Understanding that necrotizing glomerulonephritis both belongs to the spectrum of glomerulonephritis and can manifest as part of a pulmonary‑renal syndrome is essential for timely diagnosis, comprehensive evaluation of both kidney and lung pathology, and the implementation of appropriate immunosuppressive and supportive therapies to improve patient outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 2678
retrieved_docs.content: Varicose veins of the lower extremity are commonly linked to the development of قَرحَة (skin ulcer), indicating that ulcerative lesions represent a typical morphological manifestation in patients with these venous disorders. This association highlights the importance of monitoring for ulcer formation when managing lower‑extremity varicose veins.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2623
retrieved_docs.content: Necrotizing glomerulonephritis is closely linked to داء مضاد للغشاء القاعدي الكبيبي, an anti‑basement membrane disease of the kidney, and it also forms a key component of متلازمة رئية كلوية, a pulmonary‑renal syndrome that involves simultaneous injury to the lungs and kidneys. This interrelationship highlights the importance of recognizing necrotizing glomerulonephritis not only as a severe renal pathology but also as a manifestation of systemic autoimmune processes that can affect multiple organ systems, guiding comprehensive diagnostic evaluation and coordinated therapeutic strategies.
